sn7.7:1.1:
1
At Sāvatthī. Then the brahmin Suddhika Bharadvaja approached the Blessed One and exchanged greetings with him. When they had concluded their greetings and cordial talk, he sat down to one side
and recited this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:
sn7.7:2.2:
2
"In the world no brahmin is ever purified
Though he be virtuous and austere in practice;
One accomplished in knowledge and conduct is purified,
Not the others, the common folk."
3
"Even though one mutters many chants,
One does not become a brahmin by birth
If one is rotten within and defiled,
Supporting oneself by fraudulent means.
4
"Whether khattiya, brahmin, vessa, sudda,
Caṇḍala or scavenger,
If one is energetic and resolute,
Always firm in exertion,
One attains the supreme purity:
Know, O brahmin, that this is so."
sn7.7:2.3:
5
When this was said, the brahmin Suddhika Bharadvaja said to the Blessed One: "Magnificent, Master Gotama!" … And the Venerable Bharadvaja became one of the arahants.
